author: Catverter
description: Funny time units used directly and by speed ratios.

it.description: Unità di tempo buffe usate direttamente e nei rapporti di velocità.

tail-flick.name: Scatto di coda
tail-flick.description: Il minimo intervallo osservabile di disapprovazione felina.
tail-flick.scientificNote: La misurazione richiede una stanza tranquilla e una decisione umana inaccettabile.

developer-hour.name: Ora sviluppatore
developer-hour.description: La quantità di lavoro produttivo prima di riorganizzare la struttura del progetto.
developer-hour.scientificNote: Include una breve deviazione per dare il nome corretto alle cose.

happy-cat-wink.name: Occhiolino di gatto felice
happy-cat-wink.description: Una minuscola finestra temporale di approvazione felina inaspettata.

units.name: Tail flick
units.description: The minimum observable interval of feline disapproval.
units.scientificNote: Measurement requires a calm room and one unacceptable human decision.

units.name: Cat-year
units.description: One complete indoor/outdoor indecision cycle.
units.scientificNote: The subject must request exit, reject exit, then request exit again.

units.name: Snack request
units.description: The time between noticing a human and requesting food.
units.scientificNote: Hungry specimens may compress local time.

units.name: Developer hour
units.description: The amount of productive work before reorganizing the project structure.
units.scientificNote: Includes one brief detour into naming things correctly.

units.name: Meeting
units.description: A conventional administrative time sink.
units.scientificNote: Measured from first agenda item to first mention of follow-up.

units.name: Angry cat wink
units.description: The shortest measurable expression of feline disappointment.
units.scientificNote: Usually followed by complete social isolation.

units.name: Happy cat wink
units.description: A slow blink universally recognized as affection.
units.scientificNote: Researchers agree it lasts forever.

units.name: Hunger after eating
units.description: Average delay before pretending starvation again.
units.scientificNote: Exceptionally repeatable.

units.name: Programmer minute
units.description: The time required to fix one tiny issue.
units.scientificNote: Approximately one real hour.

units.name: Microwave second
units.description: A second experienced while staring through the microwave door.
units.scientificNote: Subjectively much longer than SI seconds.

units.name: Loading bar minute
units.description: Time perceived while staring at a loading indicator.
units.scientificNote: Repeated clicking has no measurable benefit.

units.name: Coffee break
units.description: Nominal workplace productivity interruption.
units.scientificNote: Frequently extended for scientific discussion.

units.name: Monday morning
units.description: Longest naturally occurring temporal phenomenon.
units.scientificNote: Time dilation confirmed by office workers worldwide.

units.name: Snooze
units.description: The optimistic promise of 'just five more minutes'.
units.scientificNote: Usually observed in sequences.

units.name: Pizza delivery
units.description: Average anticipation interval before dinner.
units.scientificNote: Duration increases proportionally to hunger.

units.name: Cat nap
units.description: A short feline nap lasting approximately four hours.
units.scientificNote: Scientifically impossible to interrupt.

units.name: Netflix episode
units.description: The amount of time before saying 'just one more'.
units.scientificNote: Usually underestimated by a factor of six.

units.name: One more turn
units.description: Temporal anomaly observed in strategy games.
units.scientificNote: Commonly extends until sunrise.

units.name: Tomorrow
units.description: The preferred completion date of every procrastinator.
units.scientificNote: Never actually arrives.

units.name: Be There in Five
units.description: The universally accepted estimate for an imminent arrival.
units.scientificNote: Actual travel time remains unrelated.

units.name: Lost Sock Search
units.description: Average duration spent looking for the missing sock.
units.scientificNote: The washing machine denies all allegations.

units.name: Windows Update
units.description: Standard software maintenance interval.
units.scientificNote: Progress remains scientifically fixed at 99%.

units.name: Quick Meeting
units.description: A meeting confidently announced to last only fifteen minutes.
units.scientificNote: Observed duration averages nearly one hour.

units.name: One Last Reel
units.description: The amount of time represented by 'just one more'.
units.scientificNote: Frequently followed by forty-three additional observations.

units.name: Shower Thought
units.description: Average interval before solving yesterday's problem.
units.scientificNote: Ideas evaporate immediately after leaving the shower.

units.name: Pasta al Dente
units.description: The ideal waiting time according to every Italian grandmother.
units.scientificNote: Exceeding this value may result in disappointed facial expressions.

units.name: Steam Sale Browse
units.description: Time required to buy games that will never be played.
units.scientificNote: Library size grows independently of available free time.

units.name: IKEA Trip
units.description: Expected duration of buying one small item.
units.scientificNote: Additional furniture acquisition is statistically inevitable.

units.name: Cat Comes When Called
units.description: Theoretical unit describing the delay before a cat voluntarily responds.
units.scientificNote: No experimentally verified observation has ever been recorded.
